evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.java has 59 System.out.println calls dumping step-by-step algorithm progress, per-collocate scores, and word lists to stdout during every API request — production code behaving like a debugging session
exploreByPattern() labels two distinct code sections as 'Step 3' (lines 179-180 and 206-207), a copy-paste artifact left after refactoring
Main.showUsage() prints the entire 'Commands:' section twice verbatim — the block at lines 79-113 is duplicated again starting at line 112
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPredicates() is a pure pass-through: it forwards all parameters to explore() with no added logic, no precondition check, no rename of semantics

CollocationEntry has multi-line Javadoc on trivial 1-2 line methods: '/** Get the number of collocates. */' over size(), '/** Check if entry is empty. */' over isEmpty() — restating comments that add no information
PositionalTokenStream declares a field 'private boolean useWord = true' that is never read; fieldType string-enum does all the dispatch work — dead field from an earlier design iteration

strengths: Infrastructure modules (CollocationsBuilder, StatisticsIndexBuilder, StatisticsReader, HybridIndexer) use SLF4J logger exclusively for operational output — no debug traces or stdout flooding
Collocation and Token value types are focused records/classes with no padding, ceremonial constructors, or boilerplate wrappers
GrammarConfigLoader defensive checks (missing 'version', empty 'relations', invalid positions) are at a genuine API boundary — a file being parsed at startup — not gratuitous null-safety theater
BlackLabQueryExecutor uses logger.warn/debug for fallback and error paths rather than println, and its error handling is specific to each failure mode
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Issues are concentrated in SemanticFieldExplorer and manifest as debug-era prototype code never promoted to production quality: step-by-step stdout traces that flood any API caller, a duplicated step header left by copy-paste, and a pass-through wrapper — all classic signs of iterative LLM-assisted assembly that was never cleaned up.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: At 72, the codebase sits in mixed territory per the global anchors. The indexing and API infrastructure modules are clean and purposeful with proper logging, pushing the score above 60. However SemanticFieldExplorer — the central semantic exploration feature — reads like prototype code left in production: 59 println calls would flood stdout on every HTTP request, the duplicated Step 3 header reveals an unreviewed copy-paste, and exploreByPredicates() is pure forwarding noise. Per the 80-anchor ('a developer regularly wades through defensive overengineering, restating comments, or formulaic patterns that obscure the real logic'), SemanticFieldExplorer clearly fits that description; the rest of the codebase does not, placing the overall score between 70-75.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer dumps 59 System.out.println traces to stdout during every API exploration request
evidence: exploreByPattern() begins with System.out.println("\n=== SEMANTIC FIELD EXPLORATION ===") and then prints each collocate with its logDice score, each intermediate noun count, and full result tables to stdout
explore() duplicates this pattern with another 30+ println calls covering identical step-trace output
SemanticFieldExplorer is called from WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticFieldExplore() during HTTP request handling — every client API call produces this stdout flood
59 total System.out.println calls in the file; SLF4J logger is declared but unused in this class
issues.suggestion: Replace all System.out.println calls in SemanticFieldExplorer with logger.debug()/logger.info() using SLF4J (the dependency is already on the classpath and logger is used correctly throughout the rest of the codebase). Step-trace output at INFO or DEBUG level will be suppressible in production without code changes.

issues.summary: exploreByPattern() labels two separate code sections 'Step 3', producing confusing doubled output
evidence: Line 179: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared " + relationName + "...") — scores DiscoveredNoun list
Line 206: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared collocates...") — computes CoreAdjective frequency map
The second block is logically Step 4 (it processes the output of the first block); the duplicate label is a copy-paste artifact from an earlier method version
issues.suggestion: Renumber the second block to 'Step 4: Identifying core collocates...' to accurately reflect the algorithm stages. This also removes the duplicate comment on line 206.

issues.summary: Main.showUsage() prints the 'Commands:' help section twice verbatim
evidence: Line 79: System.out.println("Commands:") opens the first Commands block listing blacklab-index, blacklab-query, and server
Line 112: System.out.println("Commands:") opens an identical second block with the same three commands — 34 lines of duplicated println calls
The section from line 112 to ~130 is a verbatim repeat of lines 79-98, inserting a 'Commands:' heading mid-usage-string after the Examples section
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicated Commands block (lines 112-130). The Examples section at lines 100-111 can close the usage string; a single Commands block at the top is sufficient.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPredicates() is a pure forwarding wrapper with no added logic
evidence: exploreByPredicates(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) immediately calls return explore(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) with identical argument order
No argument validation, no parameter transformation, no default injection — the method simply re-routes the call
The Javadoc says 'Explore using adjectival predicates (best for abstract nouns like theory, model)' but the implementation offers nothing beyond what explore() already does
issues.suggestion: Delete exploreByPredicates() and have callers call explore() directly. If a future overload with different defaults is needed, add it with explicit defaults rather than a pass-through.

issues.summary: CollocationEntry has multi-line Javadoc on trivial 1-2 line delegating methods that purely restate the method name
evidence: '/** Get the number of collocates. */ public int size() { return collocates.size(); }' — the doc adds nothing over the method signature
'/** Check if entry is empty. */ public boolean isEmpty() { return collocates.isEmpty(); }' — identical pattern
'/** Get top N collocates. */ public List<Collocation> topN(int n) { return collocates.stream().limit(n).toList(); }' — same pattern; the parameter name 'n' already communicates this
issues.suggestion: Remove the Javadoc from size(), isEmpty(), and topN(). Reserve Javadoc for methods where the name alone does not communicate the contract (e.g., filterByLogDice where the threshold semantics are worth documenting).

root_causes: SemanticFieldExplorer was developed iteratively (likely with LLM assistance) and graduated to production without removing debug-era stdout tracing — the class was never reviewed for production readiness as a library component called from the API server
Copy-paste-then-edit development style in exploreByPattern() left duplicate step headers and a redundant pass-through wrapper that were not caught in review
